title: 1.1: Tests that file upload to a subdirectory is denied when the user lacks upload permission in that subdirectory
1.2: Tests that symlink creation at the root directory succeeds when the user has permission at root
1.3: Tests that renaming a path within the root directory succeeds when the user has rename permission at root
1.4: Tests that creating a new file in subdirectory is denied when user has only overwrite permission (not upload)
1.5: Tests that overwriting an existing file in subdirectory succeeds when user has overwrite permission
1.6: Tests that file download from subdirectory is denied when user lacks download permission there
1.7: Tests that chtimes on a file in subdirectory is denied when user lacks chtimes permission there
1.8: Tests that chtimes on a subdirectory path checks parent directory permissions and is denied when parent lacks chtimes
1.9: Tests that chtimes on a file in subdirectory succeeds when the subdirectory has chtimes permission
1.10: Tests that directory listing (ReadDir) in subdirectory is denied when user lacks list permission there
1.11: Tests that creating a subdirectory within the restricted subdirectory is denied when user lacks create_dirs permission
1.12: Tests that file deletion in a subdirectory is denied when user lacks delete permission there
1.13: Tests that listing the root directory (ReadDir on "/") succeeds when root permissions allow listing
1.15: Tests that renaming (moving) an existing path into the restricted subdirectory is denied when the user lacks the required permissions in that subdirectory
1.16: Tests that creating a symlink that places an entry inside the restricted subdirectory is denied when the user lacks the required permissions in that subdirectory
1.17: For at least one denied operation, tests assert that an error is returned
2.1: Places the subdirectory permission tests in the sftpd test file or directory
3.1: All tests use the Go standard testing package with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern Test[Feature] or TestXxx
3.3: All tests use t.Errorf or t.Fatalf for error reporting
3.4: All tests clean up created resources (users, files, directories)
4.1: Tests use getTestUser helper to create test user configuration
4.2: Tests use getSftpClient helper to establish SFTP connections
4.3: Tests use httpd.AddUser to create users via the API
